channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Leadership is not meant for nice men — Shettima
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Vice President Kashim Shettima asserts that effective leadership requires courage and tough decisions, stating that leadership is not meant for nice men.
Read More: https://punchng.com/leadership-is-not-meant-for-nice-men-shettima/

item.title: Abia urges 811 new health workers to uphold public trust
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abia State advises 811 newly recruited health workers to uphold public trust, emphasizing integrity, professionalism, and patriotism in service delivery.
Read More: https://punchng.com/abia-urges-811-new-health-workers-to-uphold-public-trust/

item.title: Police rescue middle-aged man during patrol in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra State Police rescued an unconscious man suspected to be under the influence of hard drugs during a patrol in Awka. Read more about the Anambra Poli
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-rescue-middle-aged-man-during-patrol-in-anambra/

item.title: BREAKING: A’Court sets aside judgment nullifying INEC guidelines
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Court of Appeal has set aside a Federal High Court judgment that nullified parts of INEC guidelines for the 2027 general elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-acourt-sets-aside-judgment-nullifying-inec-guidelines/

item.title: Why army’s response to Kogi school attack was delayed — Commander
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Army explains the delayed response to the Kogi school attack where gunmen abducted four, citing the school’s isolated location.
Read More: https://punchng.com/why-armys-response-to-kogi-school-attack-was-delayed-commander/

item.title: Gbajabiamila sues ‘fake’ agency DG, demands N15bn over defamation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Chief of Staff Femi Gbajabiamila files a N15bn defamation suit against Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew over alleged kickback demands. Get the full details o
Read More: https://punchng.com/gbajabiamila-sues-fake-agency-dg-demands-n15bn-over-defamation/

item.title: Police arrest two robbery suspects in Adamawa, recovers vehicle
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Adamawa State Police Command arrests two suspects for criminal conspiracy and receiving stolen property, recovering a Toyota Hilux. Learn more about the Ad
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-two-robbery-suspects-in-adamawa-recovers-vehicle/

item.title: Army tracking abductors of Kogi students, says commander
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Army is actively tracking the abductors of four persons, including two students, from a NECO exam centre in Kogi State. Learn more about the K
Read More: https://punchng.com/army-tracking-abductors-of-kogi-students-says-commander/

item.title: Which England stars could be on the move this summer?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Get the very latest transfer news from Sky Sports with a number of England players possibly set to move clubs this summer.

item.title: Court jails man for life over friend’s death in Abuja
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
An Abuja court has sentenced Ekang Bassey to life imprisonment for the death of his friend, Emeka Nnonifu, following a birthday disagreement.
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-jails-man-for-life-over-friends-death-in-abuja/

item.title: Adeboye asks scholars to develop solutions to insecurity
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Pastor Adeboye urges scholars, church leaders, and policymakers to develop practical solutions to Nigeria’s worsening insecurity, moving beyond academic di
Read More: https://punchng.com/adeboye-asks-scholars-to-develop-solutions-to-insecurity/

item.title: Arsenal agree fee to sign Greek winger Tzolis
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arsenal have agreed a deal with Club Brugge for winger Christos Tzolis, Sky Sports News understands.

item.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

item.title: Messi: Players outside Europe can win Ballon d’Or – Organisers
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Ballon d’Or organisers have dismissed the notion that footballers must play for European clubs to have a realistic chance of winning the prestigious individual prize, insisting the award is open to players from leagues across the world. As the race for the 2026 Ballon d’Or gathers moment

item.title: Falconets Begin Camping In Abuja Ahead Of Poland 2026 FIFA U-20 Women’s World Cup
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria’s U-20 women’s national team, the Falconets, have begun their training camp in Abuja as preparations intensify for the 2026 FIFA U-20 Women’s World Cup, which will be held in Poland from 5 to 27 September. Head coach Moses Aduku has invited 28 players for the initial phase of camping, with 1

item.title: US, Iran Exchange Fresh Strikes as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Escalate
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged fresh military strikes on Thursday, deepening tensions across the Middle East as both sides battled over control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz despite renewed calls for diplomacy. Iran launched retaliatory attacks against US allies in the Gulf after American

item.title: Why I dumped ADC — Salihu Lukman
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) was thrown into fresh turmoil on Wednesday after one of its leading figures, Salihu Mohammed Lukman,... The post Why I dumped ADC — Salihu Lukman appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Lights, Camera, Create! Blu Light Film & TV Academy Unveils Young Creators Summer Camp | July 27th – 31st
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: The Young Creators Summer Camp is a 5-day filmmaking experience rooted in purpose and faith. Designed specifically for aspiring young storytellers, this action-packed summer camp offers direct mentorship from multi-award-winning filmmaker, Michelle Bello, alongside top industry professionals. Date:

item.title: Lawal Ibrahim: No court declared Colleen Mero Yesufu owner, yet EFCC seeks to forfeit plot 4022 for her
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Since Colleen’s adverse claim emerged in 2023, the dispute has escalated into EFCC investigations, prosecution, forfeiture proceedings and occupation of Rebecca’s residence. Now, despite a Federal High Court order directing the parties to maintain status quo ante bellum, Rebecca says she remains wit

item.title: Oriire postmortem: Who is afraid of the truth?
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A nation rises or falls depending on the knowledge of its leaders. When those entrusted with power lack the understanding of the offices they hold, decisions become guesses, policies become experiments The post Oriire postmortem: Who is afraid of the truth? appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Messi overtakes Mbappe in top scorer race
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Argentina captain Lionel Messi has moved ahead of France forward Kylian Mbappe in the race for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Golden Boot after helping his side book a place in the final. The post 2026 World Cup: Messi overtakes Mbappe in top scorer race appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Cross River Clears 5-Month Obudu Ranch Salary Arrears After Workers’ Protest
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Cross River State Government has cleared five months of salary arrears owed to workers of the Obudu Ranch Resort, following a peaceful protest staged by the workers on July 1 over non-payment. The payment was confirmed in a press statement issued by the Special Adviser to the Governor on Obudu R

item.title: Panic in Plateau community as beheaded woman’s body found in refuse dump
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Golok Nanmwa, JOS Residents of Angwan Durun-Uwa community in Barkin Ladi Local Government Area of Plateau State were thrown into panic on Thursday following the discovery of the decomposing body of a beheaded woman inside a sack at a refuse dump. The identity of the deceased was yet to be ascerta

item.title: Fire Kills 11 at Algeria Orphanage Amid Heatwave
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: At least 11 people, including several children, have died after a fire engulfed an orphanage in Algeria’s capital, Algiers, as the country grapples with a severe heatwave that has fuelled hundreds of wildfires. The blaze broke out before dawn on Thursday at the orphanage located in the Mohamma

item.title: Canada's air ranks as worst in the world due to wildfire smoke
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Canada has 835 active wildfires - including 112 that are considered to be burning out of control. The smoke has caused hazardous air quality conditions - with Toronto residents neging warned to stay indoors if possible. US officials are concerned that the smog could spread into the northeastern part

item.title: Done Deal: Nigerian Youngster Joins French Club Stade Reims
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Ligue 2 club Stade de Reims have announced the signing of Nigerian youngster John Otomewo, Completesports.com reports. Otomewo, who linked up with Reims from Israeli outfit Hapoel Jerusalem penned a contract until 2031. The defensive midfielder will be able to rely on the presence of fellow Nigerian

item.title: What Nigeria Stocks Tell Investors About Africa Markets: Live Q&A
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about Nigeria’s world-beating stocks, and where investors are finding opportunities in African markets, in a Live Q&A on Friday, July 17 at 12 p.m. GMT

item.title: Tella Targets Super Eagles Return
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Bayer Leverkusen Nigerian forward Nathan Tella has expressed his desire to return the Super Eagles. Tella has not featured for the Super Eagles since the 2025 Unity Cup, with injuries limiting his involvement during with the team. Since making his debut in 2023, the 27-year-old has made just two app

item.title: US set to sell $2bn weapons to Saudi Arabia
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The United States has approved a proposed weapons sale worth nearly $2 billion to Saudi Arabia, a move aimed at strengthening the kingdom’s air defence capabilities amid heightened tensions across the Middle East. The post US set to sell $2bn weapons to Saudi Arabia appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Wizkid confirmed as headliner for major US summer festivals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He added that he will also perform at Trillville, also known as Tribeville Festival Deluxe,in Minneapolis on July 24.

item.title: Senate confirms Fasina as non-career ambassador
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Amid protests on the floor on Thursday, the Senate confirmed the nomination of Professor Abayomi Sunday Fasina by President Bola Tinubu... The post Senate confirms Fasina as non-career ambassador appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Nigeria youth international Shakirat Moshood has completed a move to Portuguese top-flight club SCU Torreense on a two-year contract, Soccernet.ng reports. The highly rated Super Falcons prospect leaves Nigeria Women Football League champions Bayelsa Queens to begin her first professional adventure

item.title: How I recovered from nationality switch failure – Favour Ofili
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigerian sprinter Favour Ofili says therapy, support from close friends and her coach helped her recover from one of the toughest periods of her career.

item.title: Insecurity: Sokoto Police Kill Bandits,Recover Arms, Stolen Cattle In Fresh Operations
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Sokoto State Police Command said it has neutralize some suspected Lakurawa bandits, kidnappers and cattle rustlers, among other criminals. The State Commissioner of Police (CP), Alhaji Hayatu Hassan Shaffa, disclosed this on Thursday in Sokoto at a press conference at police headquarters, Sokoto

item.title: As the war rekindles, what options are left to the US and Iran?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US and Iran have rekindled their tit-for-tat strikes as both battle over control of the Strait of Hormuz. It comes a month after a preliminary deal was signed to end the conflict. Kuwait and Bahrain faced renewed aerial attacks from Iran, with Theran saying it was targeting US military bases in

item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has commenced the process of deregistering 100,000 companies... The post CAC commences process to deregister 100,000 companies over annual tax returns regulations appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigerian streamer and content creator, Oderhohwo Joseph Efe, better known as Carter Efe has taken to her social media page to express disappointment after his visa application was denied. Speaking during a livestream, he shared his frustration with the difficulties of travelling abroad, feeling trap

item.title: Google Ordered to Open Android Features, Search Data to AI Rivals Under EU Rules
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: The European Commission has ordered Google to open 11 Android features and share anonymised search data with OpenAI and other AI competitors under new Digital Markets Act requirements aimed at boosting competition. The post Google Ordered to Open Android Features, Search Data to AI Rivals Under EU R

item.title: Genoa bridge collapse trial highlights Italy's ageing infrastructure issue
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: An Italian court sentenced 32 defendants for their role in a deadly bridge disaster eight years ago that killed 43 people in Genoa. One of the accused was Autostrade's former chief executive, Giovanni Castellucci, was found guilty of vehicular homicide and negligence and was sentenced to twelve year

item.title: Meet the cigarette-smoking fan favourite who grabbed shock Open lead again
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Fans’ favourite Dan Brown is hoping his mid-round smoking will be allowed to continue after grabbing a shock share of the early clubhouse lead at The Open.

item.title: What I know about Mary Habila’s death – Close friend
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Mary told me she was tired and wanted to take a shower before sleeping. That was the last time I saw her alive,” Baaki stated.

item.description: By Golok Nanmwa, JOS Plateau State Governor, Caleb Mutfwang, has urged the Nigerian Army to approach security challenges in Plateau and other parts of the country as combat operations against threats to national sovereignty rather than conventional peacekeeping missions. item.description: The Police Service Commission (PSC) has concluded the recruitment of 50,000 Police Constables into the Nigeria Police Force (NPF) after publishing the names of successful applicants. item.title: What Makes Real-Money Pokies So Popular
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Real-money pokies deliver a high-energy, versatile escape accessible, visually striking, mechanically inventive, and backed by the most generous promotions in digital gambling. Played responsibly within a fixed budget, they offer genuinely thrilling, on-demand entertainment.

item.description: The Oyo State Commissioner for Information, Mr Dotun Oyelade, has said the two surveillance aircraft procured by the state government would arrive by the end of July. Oyelade, in a

item.title: Ukraine: Protests erupt after defence minister sacked
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: With demonstrators shouting “Shame!” and “Power to the people!”, rare protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on Thursday over the dismissal of the country’s popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Seen as a moderniser, Fedorov was removed by President Volodymyr Zelensky after only six month

The Plateau State Comptroller of the NIS, CIS Mary Abang, disc

Gbajabiamila sued Adeyemi for alleged defamation. In the suit filed before a Federal Capital Territory

item.description: The PDP in Bayelsa State has again launched a broadside against the APC-led government following its defence of the ₦100 billion stadium project under construction at Igbogene in Yenagoa. Justice Mohammed Umar gave the warning after the Department of State Services (DSS)’s lawyer, Akinlolu Kehinde, SAN, made the oral

item.title: Faena Condo Project Sells $30 Million Penthouse on Miami River
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Argentine fashion entrepreneur-turned-developer Alan Faena sold a $30 million penthouse in his planned twin condo towers on the Miami River, a milestone in the mission to take his maximalist, theatrical brand worldwide.

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: His blossoming, botanical and flowing forms recall the life cycles of plants and humans. Designer Ali Zalzali tells FRANCE 24 about creating his first couture collection, ‘Natura Morta’ and the evocative short film he made to conjure up the universe of these hand-crafted pieces.

item.title: ‎FAAN to stop use of physical ID for domestic travel
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) is rolling out V-Pass, a biometric identity verification system designed to make domestic air travel faster, safer and more seamless. The post ‎FAAN to stop use of physical ID for domestic travel appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Ebola Spreading In DRC ‘Faster Than Any Previous Outbreak,’ WHO Chief Warns
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: More than 2,000 cases including 796 deaths have been confirmed in the DR Congo since the outbreak was declared on May 15, making it "now the third-largest Ebola outbreak on record", WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus told reporters, warning that "in the past month, it had expanded faster than any

item.title: 19-Year-Old Remanded For Impregnating 14-Year-Old Anambra Girl
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: An Anambra State Children, Sexual and Gender-Based Violence Offences Court, Awka, on Thursday ordered the remand of a 19-year-old Chibuike Ikemelu, for allegedly defiling and impregnating a 14-year-old girl. Ikemelu is facing a one-count charge of defilement. The prosecutor, Insp Chinyere Okechukwu,

item.title: Troops arrest 24 foreign nationals in Lagos hideout raid
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: As part of ongoing efforts to rid the 81 Division Area of Responsibility (AOR) of criminal elements, illegal immigrants and other security threats, troops of 65 Battalion have arrested 24 foreign nationals during a coordinated raid on a suspected hideout in Epe Local Government Area of Lagos State.

item.title: BREAKING: Gbaja sues PFIPC’s Adeniyi Adeyemi for N15bn over alleged defamation
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Femi Gbajabiamila, chief of staff to President Bola Tinubu, has sued Adeniyi Adeyemi, director-general of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), for alleged defamation. In the suit filed before a Federal Capital Territory (FCT) high court, Gbajabiamila is seek

item.title: Unchecked exploitation, mining threaten Nigeria’s economy, ex-minister warns
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Minister of State for Environment Ibrahim Usman Jibril has raised concerns over unchecked exploitation and mining, warning that the trend could affect the country’s economy. The post Unchecked exploitation, mining threaten Nigeria’s economy, ex-minister warns appeared first on The Sun Nigeria

item.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

item.title: Obudu Ranch Resort: Otu clears outstanding salaries, vows prompt payment
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Cross River State Government has paid five months’ salary arrears owed to workers at the Obudu Ranch Resort. The post Obudu Ranch Resort: Otu clears outstanding salaries, vows prompt payment appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Abefeholic Lyrics by Abefe
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Abefe Lyrics Abefeholics by Abefe is a celebratory anthem that captures the artist's confidence, gratitude, and outlook on life, reflecting on a journey marked by challenges and perseverance, the lyrics acknowledge God's blessings while embracing success, financial freedom, and self-belief. The song

item.title: Alleged Plot to Attack Abuja Schools: Court Denies Bail to Suspect Arrested By DSS
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has denied bail to a man arrested by the Department of State Services (DSS), for allegedly planning multiple attacks on several schools in the

item.title: Kano gov distributes 1,540 plots, N100m to APC grassroots leaders
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kano State Governor, Alhaji Abba Kabir Yusuf, has distributed 1,540 plots of land and a cash grant of ₦100 million to leaders of the APC... The post Kano gov distributes 1,540 plots, N100m to APC grassroots leaders appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NLC secures breakthrough as warring NURTW factions agree to end crisis
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Victor Ahiuma-Young ABUJA — The prolonged leadership crisis rocking the National Union of Road Transport Workers, NURTW, may be nearing an end following a major breakthrough brokered by the Nigeria Labour Congress, NLC. This followed a peace meeting brokered by the President of Nigeria Labour Con

item.title: Protests in Ukrainian cities against Zelensky's removal of defence minister
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Ukraine's president has not explained Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal, which is causing great upset among civil society and the military.

item.title: Abbott, Up 14%, Strikes Three-Month High After Boosting 2026 Profit Outlook
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Strong Results Reinforce Micron Technology’s (MU) Importance in AI Infrastructure Buildout
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: How World Cup football jerseys became political fashion in Latin America
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In both Brazil and Colombia, far-right political movements have coopted the national football team's jersey. But why?

item.title: BREAKING: Atiku Demands Umahi’s Suspension Over Nurse Mary Habila’s Death
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) Atiku Abubakar has demanded the immediate suspension of the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, over the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila, who died within the Minister’s private residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State, on June 27. In

item.title: DR Congo's healthcare workers go on strike as Ebola epidemic spreads
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Health workers fighting the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo are going on strike over unpaid wages. This comes as the World Health Organization warned that Ebola was spreading in the Democratic Republic of Congo at a faster pace than during any previous outbreak of the deadly virus. More than 2,000 Ebola

item.title: Epstein linked to Israeli ‘deep state’, US intel, says Vance: What we know
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In podcast interview, Vance also acknowledges Trump administration 'screwed up' on communications around Epstein files.

item.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Tops Q2 Targets On Leading-Edge Chip Demand
